title: Artificial Intelligence and Public Standards: government response to report
description: The government's response to the Committee's 2020 report Artificial Intelligence and Public Standards
summary: The government has responded to a report on Artificial Intelligence and Public Standards, outlining its approach to the development and regulation of AI in the public sector. The response aims to address concerns raised by the committee's 2020 report and sets out the government's plans for managing AI's impact on public standards.

title: Report and impact assessment on Copyright and Artificial Intelligence
description: A report and impact assessment on the use of copyright works in the development of artificial intelligence (AI) systems.
summary: This report examines the usage of copyrighted materials in the development of artificial intelligence (AI) systems. It also assesses the potential impact of this use on copyright laws and industries affected.

title: Generative artificial intelligence (AI) in education
description: The Department for Education’s (DfE) position on the use of generative AI in the education sector.
summary: This policy document outlines the Department for Education's stance on using generative AI in educational settings, including its potential benefits and risks. It likely provides guidance for educators, schools, and other stakeholders on responsible AI usage in the education sector.

title: Transport artificial intelligence action plan
description: Sets out how DfT will work with the transport sector to exploit the opportunities from AI while managing potential risks.
summary: The Transport AI Action Plan is a government strategy to utilize artificial intelligence in the transport industry. It aims to balance the potential benefits of AI in transport with the potential risks.

title: Copyright and artificial intelligence: progress report
description: A progress report on the use of copyright works in the development of AI systems, under Section 137 of the Data (Use and Access) Act.
summary: This is a report detailing the use of copyrighted material in creating AI systems, following the requirements of a specific data access law. The progress report aims to address the implications of using copyrighted works in developing artificial intelligence.

title: The regulation of artificial intelligence as a medical device: government response to the RHC
description: Government response to the recommendations made by the Regulatory Horizons Council (RHC) on the regulation of AI as a medical device.
summary: The government has provided a response to the Regulatory Horizons Council's recommendations on the regulation of AI as a medical device. The response outlines the government's plan to address the RHC's suggestions and implement new regulations for the use of AI in medical treatments.

title: ​​Ofqual’s approach to regulating the use of artificial intelligence in the qualifications sector​
description: Published in response to a request from the Secretary of State for Education and the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology.
summary: Ofqual is developing a policy to regulate the use of artificial intelligence in qualifications to ensure its integrity and fairness. This policy aims to address concerns around AI's potential impact on the qualifications sector, in response to a request from the relevant government ministers.

title: Government response to the House of Lords Select Committee on Artificial Intelligence
description: Government response to the Lords Select Committee on Artificial Intelligence report ‘AI in the UK: No Room for Complacency’.
summary: The UK government has responded to the House of Lords Select Committee on Artificial Intelligence's report highlighting concerns about AI use in the country. The response aims to address these concerns and promote responsible AI development and use in the UK.

title: AI and Public Standards – Terms of Reference
description: Terms of Reference and FAQs for the Committee's review into artificial intelligence and public standards.
summary: The AI policy document outlines the terms of reference and frequently asked questions for a committee reviewing artificial intelligence and its impact on public standards. The committee will likely examine how AI affects existing standards and regulations in the public sphere.

title: CSPL submission to Science and Technology Committee inquiry on Governance of AI
description: CSPL has submitted written evidence to the Science and Technology Committee's inquiry on Governance of Artificial Intelligence.
summary: The Committee on Standards in Public Life (CSPL) has submitted evidence to an inquiry on the governance of artificial intelligence. This submission likely outlines their views and recommendations on responsible AI governance.
